violations.description: Clean-in-place (CIP) equipment - Design and construction. — --Nonfood contact surface of clean in place equipment was not cleaned properly. - CIP equipment require a more thorough or more frequent cleanings.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. - Food contact surfaces in glazing area were not cleaned from morning production. - Outside and surroundings of dishwasher in need of cleaning.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Surface of equipment/utensil did not reach minimum temperature of 160F during hot water sanitize rinse of warewash machine. - Large dishwasher tested three times and the highest temped at 121.4*F during inspection.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. - Floors walls and ceiling require a more frequent cleaning.
Food supplies. — --Food item obtained from unapproved source. - Jelly is being sold that comes from an unapproved source.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. - Many food items being sold were not dated. Examples: Pizzas, Pies, Breads, Sweet Goods in front customer area. All food items being sold need to be dated. - Containers in freezers and coolers holding food items not labeled or dated either.

Variance requirement for specialized processing methods. — --Food establishment did not obtain approved variance or HACCP plan from regulatory authority for special process of. - Tuna steaks are being sold to customers in front freezer that are vacuum sealed. No date or labels of source.

Clean-in-place (CIP) equipment - Design and construction. — --Nonfood contact surface of clean in place equipment was not cleaned properly. - CIP equipment require a more thorough or more frequent cleanings.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. - Food contact surfaces in glazing area were not cleaned from morning production.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. - Walk-In Freezer floors in need of cleaning.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Surface of equipment/utensil did not reach minimum temperature of 160F during hot water sanitize rinse of warewash machine. - Large dishwasher tested at 125.8*F during inspection.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Attachments to walls and ceilings (e.g. light fixtures, vent covers, decorations etc..) were not cleanable and/or maintained in good repair. - Ceiling fan behind counter in need of maintenance. Fan surrounded by damaged tiles.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. - Floors walls and ceiling require a more frequent cleaning.--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. - Floors walls and ceiling throughout require repairs.

General premises. — --Opening to outside or portion of building that is not part of the food establishment, was not protected against the entry of insects and rodents. - Screen door in the back of establishment, in room that holds large bags of dry goods, has gaps leading to outside.
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean, employee dressing area, cleaning equipment storage, living/sleeping quarters separate (1 pt deducted)
